message.content:
depend on My animy list site
compare to my project wwhat feature i could add to improve UX

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so m…
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the …
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/…
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v…
- omarchy: REQUIRED for end-user customization of Linux desktop, window manager, or system config. Use when editing ~/.config/hypr/, ~/.config/waybar/, ~/.config/walker/, ~/.config/alacritty/, ~/.config/kitty/, ~/.config/ghostty/, ~/.config/mako/, or ~/.config…
- teach: Switches Claude into teaching mode. Instead of writing code or giving solutions,
Claude acts as an infinitely patient tutor — guiding the user to solve the problem
themselves through questions, explanations, and hints.

content.text: Let me explore your current project and research MyAnimeList's features to give you a solid comparison.

input.prompt:
Explore this anime showcase project thoroughly. I need to understand every user-facing feature currently implemented. Look at:

1. All HTML pages (public/index.html, public/search/index.html, public/detail/index.html)
2. All frontend JS files (public/app.js, public/search/app.js, public/detail/app.js)
3. All CSS files
4. Backend routes in src/router.js
5. Controllers in src/controllers/
6. Database schema in src/schema.sql
7. Any authentication/user features (login, register, favorites)

Report a comprehensive list of every feature the app currently has from a user's perspective. Be thorough - check for things like pagination, filtering, sorting, error handling, responsive design, loading states, etc.

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me create a detailed report of all user-facing features:

## Comprehensive Feature Report: Anime Showcase Application

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here are **all the user-facing features** currently implemented in this anime showcase project:

### CORE PAGES & NAVIGATION

1. **Homepage** (`/`)
   - Navigation brand with title "Anime Showcase" and tagline "Discover top anime and manga"
   - Search bar at top with dropdown to select type (anime/manga) and text input
   - Two horizontal scrolling sections displaying top 10 anime and top 10 manga
   - Cards are clickable to navigate to detail page
   - Loading indicator while fetching

2. **Search Page** (`/search/:entityType?q=...`)
   - Same persistent search bar as homepage
   - Type selector (anime/manga) that reflects current search type
   - Search query pre-filled in input from URL parameter
   - Results displayed in responsive grid layout
   - "No results found" message when search returns empty
   - Card-based display with lazy-loaded images

3. **Detail/Entity Page** (`/:entityType/:id`)
   - Same persistent search bar
   - Large detail card with cover image on right side (image on left on mobile)
   - Title with gradient styling
   - Meta information grid showing: Score, Type, Status, Episodes/Chapters/Volumes (depending on entity type), Year (if available), Rating (if available)
   - Genre badges with special styling
   - Full synopsis in scrollable section
   - Save to Favorites button with feedback states (changes to "Saved!" or "Failed" on response)
   - Responsive layout (side-by-side on desktop, stacked on mobile)

4. **Login Page** (`/login/`)
   - Simple form with username and password inputs
   - Submit button
   - Error message display area
   - Redirects to homepage on successful login

5. **Register Page** (`/register/`)
   - Form with username and password input fields
   - Submit button
   - Error message display area
   - Redirects to login page on successful registration

6. **Favorites Page** (`/favorites/`)
   - Displays user's saved anime/manga items
   - Each favorite shows as a link with delete button
   - Error message for unauthenticated users ("please log in to see favorites")
   - Empty state message ("no favorites yet") when no items saved
   - Delete functionality with live removal from page

---

### SEARCH & FILTERING FEATURES

1. **Type Selection**: Dropdown allowing users to toggle between Anime and Manga searches
2. **Search Bar**: Text input to search by title/query
3. **Search Submission**: Form submission with validation (requires non-empty query)
4. **Pagination Support**:
   - Previous/Next buttons with disable states
   - Current page indicator
   - Page navigation via URL parameters
5. **Search Results**: Grid layout with responsive columns

---

### DETAIL FEATURES

1. **Dynamic Content Display**:
   - Title, rating (with star icon), type, status
   - Episodes count for anime, chapters and volumes for manga
   - Year and rating (when available from API)
   - Genre badges with hover/selection styling
   - Full synopsis with overflow scrolling
   - Cover image with shadow effects

2. **Interactive Elements**:
   - Clickable cards on all pages to navigate to details
   - Save to Favorites button with state feedback
   - Navigation back via search bar

---

### AUTHENTICATION & USER FEATURES

1. **User Registration**:
   - Username and password input validation
   - Password minimum length requirement (8 characters)
   - Duplicate username detection with error message
   - Successful registration redirects to login

2. **User Login**:
   - Username/password authentication
   - Invalid credentials error handling
   - JWT token generation stored as httpOnly cookie
   - Post-login redirect to homepage

3. **Favorites System** (authenticated users only):
   - Add anime/manga to favorites from detail page
   - View all saved favorites on dedicated page
   - Delete favorites with real-time UI removal
   - User-scoped (each user has their own favorites list)
   - Database validation (entity_type must be anime or manga)

---

### API ENDPOINTS & DATA FEATURES

1. **Search API**: `/api/:entityType/search?q=...&page=...`
   - Query parameter validation
   - Pagination support with has_next_page flag
   - Current page tracking
   - Response includes all anime/manga details

2. **Top Lists API**: `/api/:entityType/top`
   - Returns top 10 anime or manga
   - Caching middleware (15-minute TTL)

3. **Details API**: `/api/:entityType/:id`
   - Fetches complete details for single anime/manga
   - Caching enabled

4. **Favorites API**:
   - GET `/api/favorites` - fetch user's favorites (auth required)
   - POST `/api/favorites` - add favorite (auth required)
   - DELETE `/api/favorites/:id` - remove favorite (auth required)

5. **Auth API**:
   - POST `/api/auth/register` - user registration
   - POST `/api/auth/login` - user login with JWT

---

### DESIGN & UX FEATURES

1. **Dark Theme Design**:
   - Firefox-inspired dark color scheme
   - Custom CSS variables for consistent theming
   - Accent color (orange) with gradient effects
   - Smooth transitions and animations

2. **Responsive Design**:
   - Mobile-first responsive layout
   - Sticky search bar at top
   - Responsive grid layouts (grid changes columns based on viewport)
   - Detail page layout switches from side-by-side to stacked on mobile (<700px)
   - Horizontal scroll for top lists on desktop

3. **Visual Feedback**:
   - Card hover effects (lift animation, border color change, shadow)
   - Button hover states with opacity and scale
   - Loading text indicators
   - Disabled pagination buttons with reduced opacity
   - Button state changes after actions (Save to Favorites feedback)

4. **Typography & Colors**:
   - Inter font family
   - Gradient text for titles
   - Color-coded meta information (accent colors for ratings)
   - Star icons for ratings

5. **Scrolling & Overflow**:
   - Horizontal scroll for top lists with snap alignment
   - Scrollbar styling for synopsis sections
   - Custom scrollbar appearance

---

### TECHNICAL FEATURES

1. **Performance**:
   - Lazy loading for images
   - LRU caching middleware (max 1000 items, 15-minute TTL)
   - Monkey-patched response caching via res.json override

2. **Error Handling**:
   - Form validation with error messages
   - API error responses with helpful messages
   - Upstream error handling (for Jikan API failures)
   - 400 status for invalid entity types
   - 401 status for unauthorized access
   - 404 status for missing favorites
   - 409 status for duplicate username registration
   - Centralized error middleware

3. **Security**:
   - Password hashing with bcrypt (10 salt rounds)
   - JWT authentication with httpOnly cookies
   - SQL injection protection (using parameterized queries)
   - Entity type validation (only anime/manga allowed)

4. **Data Validation**:
   - Username presence check
   - Password length validation (minimum 8 characters)
   - Entity type whitelist validation
   - Required field checks (entity_id, entity_type for favorites)

---

### DATA DISPLAY FEATURES

Each card displays:
- **Image**: Poster/cover art with 3:4 aspect ratio
- **Title**: Truncated with ellipsis on overflow
- **Synopsis**: Snippet with max-height and scrollbar
- **Meta**: Rating (with star) and Episode/Chapter count
- **Score**: Color-coded in accent color
- **Episodes/Chapters**: Formatted display (e.g., "12 eps", "? ch")

Detail pages display:
- Large cover image
- Full title with gradient
- Complete metadata grid
- All genres as badges
- Full synopsis with scroll support
- Year and rating (when available)

---

### URL STRUCTURE

- `/` - Home page
- `/login/` - Login page
- `/register/` - Registration page
- `/favorites/` - Favorites list
- `/search/:entityType?q=...&page=...` - Search results
- `/:entityType/:id` - Detail page (anime/123 or manga/456)

All pages maintain the search bar for easy navigation across the app.

Now let me research MyAnimeList's features.

input.prompt: List every user-facing feature on MyAnimeList's homepage and navigation. Include: navigation menu items, search features, content sections, filtering/sorting options, user features, community features, list management features, recommendation features, and any other notable UX features. Be comprehensive.

List every user-facing feature on this MyAnimeList anime detail page. Include: content displayed (metadata, stats, images, trailers), interactive elements (ratings, reviews, recommendations), navigation features, related content sections, community features, and any other notable UX elements. Be comprehensive.

content.content:
# MyAnimeList Homepage Features

## Navigation Menu
- **Anime**: Search, Top Anime, Seasonal Anime, Videos, Reviews, Recommendations, 2026 Challenge, Fantasy Anime League
- **Manga**: Search, Top Manga, Adapted to Anime, Manga Store, Reviews, Recommendations, 2026 Challenge
- **Community**: Interest Stacks, Forums, Clubs, Blogs, Users
- **Industry**: News, Featured Articles, People, Characters, Companies, MAL×Japan
- **Watch**: Episode Videos, Anime Trailers
- **Read**: Manga Store
- **Help**: About, Support, Advertising, FAQ, Report, Staff, MAL Supporter

## Search & Discovery
- Global search bar with result filtering by content type (anime, manga, characters, people, companies, stores, clubs, users, news, forums)
- Advanced anime/manga search pages
- Seasonal anime browsing
- Top anime/manga rankings with filtering options

## Content Sections
- Spring 2026 seasonal anime showcase
- Latest episode videos with episode-level linking
- Most popular anime trailers
- Anime & manga news feed
- Featured articles section
- Latest user reviews and recommendations

## User Account Features
- "Sign Up" and "Login" options
- User profile links
- Personal anime/manga list management (add to list functionality)
- Membership/Supporter tier

## Sorting & Filtering
- Content ranked by score, popularity, recency
- Airing vs. upcoming vs. all-time filters
- Media type indicators (TV, Movie, ONA, etc.)
- Episode count and member count displays

## Community Features
- Recent forum discussions with reply counts
- User blogs and interest stacks
- Club browsing and creation
- User recommendations with reasoning

## Additional Features
- Dark mode support
- Mobile apps (iOS/Android)
- Social media integration links
- Manga store integration (Mangamirai partnership)
- Advertisement placement areas

content.content:
# MyAnimeList Cowboy Bebop Page - User-Facing Features

## Content Display
- **Metadata**: Title, Japanese alternative name, type (TV), episode count (26), airing dates (Apr 3, 1998 - Apr 24, 1999), broadcast schedule, and production credits
- **Statistics**: Score (8.75), ranking (#50), popularity (#41), member count (2,050,505), favorites (89,437)
- **Synopsis**: Plot summary describing the bounty hunter crew's adventures in 2071
- **Background Information**: Historical context about initial broadcast censorship and international reception
- **Genre/Theme Tags**: Action, Award Winning, Sci-Fi, Adult Cast, Space

## Media Elements
- Opening and ending theme information with preview controls and music streaming links (Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon Music, YouTube)
- Character images and voice actor listings
- Series poster/cover art
- Episode preview video embedded

## Interactive Features
- "Add to My List" with status options (Watching, Completed, On-Hold, Dropped, Plan to Watch)
- Episode progress tracker
- Personal scoring dropdown (1-10 scale with descriptive labels)
- "Add to Favorites" functionality
- Social sharing buttons (Facebook, X, Reddit, Tumblr)

## Navigation & Related Content
- Breadcrumb navigation
- Tabbed navigation: Details, Characters & Staff, Episodes, Videos, Stats, Reviews, Recommendations, Interest Stacks, News, Forum, Clubs, Pictures
- "Related Entries" section showing manga adaptations and spin-offs
- Related anime recommendations with user counts
- Interest Stacks curated by community

## Community Features
- User review section displaying multiple perspectives with helpful/unhelpful voting
- Forum discussion threads with reply counts
- Club listings
- Recent news articles mentioning the series
- Featured articles with view counts

## External Resources
- Official website link
- Streaming platform availability (Crunchyroll, Netflix, Tubi TV)
- External database links (AniDB, ANN, Wikipedia, Syoboi)
- Production company information with clickable links

## Additional UX Elements
- Login/Sign-up prompts for account-dependent features
- Dark mode toggle indicator
- Advertisement sections
- Footer navigation and legal links
- Mobile app promotion badges

content.text:
Here's a comparison of your project vs MyAnimeList, with practical features you could add given your tech constraints (vanilla JS, no frameworks, no async/await):

---

## What You Already Have
- Top anime/manga lists, search with pagination, detail pages
- User auth (register/login), favorites with add/delete
- Dark theme, responsive layout, caching

---

## Features You Could Add (ranked by UX impact)

### 1. **Watch Status Tracking** (high impact)
MAL's core feature is tracking anime status: *Watching, Completed, Plan to Watch, Dropped, On-Hold*. Your favorites table could be extended with a `status` column and an `episode_progress` field. This turns your app from a "search tool" into a "personal tracker."

### 2. **Personal Scoring**
Let users rate anime 1-10 from the detail page. Store it alongside favorites. Display the user's score on their favorites page. Simple DB column addition + a `<select>` dropdown.

### 3. **Seasonal / Airing Anime Section**
Jikan has `GET /v4/seasons/now` — you could add a "Currently Airing" section on the homepage. MAL makes this a primary discovery path.

### 4. **Related Anime on Detail Page**
Jikan's detail response includes `relations` data (sequels, prequels, spin-offs). Display these as clickable links on the detail page. MAL does this prominently and it keeps users exploring.

### 5. **Character & Staff Info**
Jikan has `GET /v4/anime/{id}/characters` and `/staff`. A tab or collapsible section on the detail page showing main characters with images would add depth.

### 6. **Trailer / Video Embed**
Jikan returns `trailer.youtube_id` in anime data. Embed a YouTube iframe on the detail page — high engagement, minimal effort.

### 7. **Advanced Search Filters**
MAL lets users filter by: genre, type (TV/Movie/OVA), status (airing/completed), score range, year. Jikan supports these as query params (`type`, `status`, `min_score`, `genres`). Add filter dropdowns above your search results.

### 8. **Sort Options for Search Results**
Jikan supports `order_by` (score, popularity, rank, title) and `sort` (asc/desc). Add a sort dropdown to the search page.

### 9. **Favorites Page Improvements**
- Group favorites by status (Watching / Completed / etc.)
- Show the user's personal score next to each entry
- Show episode progress (e.g., "8/24 episodes")
- Add cover image thumbnails instead of plain text links

### 10. **Recommendations Section**
Jikan has `GET /v4/anime/{id}/recommendations`. Show "Users who liked this also liked..." on the detail page.

### 11. **Streaming Availability**
Jikan returns `streaming` links (Crunchyroll, Netflix, etc.) in detail data. Display these as external links on the detail page.

### 12. **Statistics Display**
Show MAL community stats on your detail page: rank, popularity, members count, favorites count — all available in the Jikan response already. You may already show `score`, but rank/popularity/members adds social proof.

### 13. **Search Type Expansion**
MAL lets you search characters and people, not just anime/manga. Jikan supports `GET /v4/characters?q=...` and `GET /v4/people?q=...`.

## Quick Wins (smallest effort, noticeable UX boost)

| Feature | Effort | Jikan endpoint |
|---|---|---|
| Trailer embed | ~10 lines JS | Already in detail response |
| Streaming links | ~15 lines JS | Already in detail response |
| Related anime links | ~20 lines JS | Already in detail response |
| Sort dropdown on search | ~30 lines JS + minor backend | `order_by` param |
| Seasonal anime section | ~1 new route + frontend section | `/v4/seasons/now` |

## What I'd Skip

- Forums/clubs/blogs — too complex, needs real community scale
- Interest stacks — curation-heavy, low ROI
- News feed — requires editorial content
- Social sharing buttons — low engagement for small projects
